Oracc: HBTIN — Hellenistic Babylonia: Texts, Iconography, Names

YOS 20, 100 [List of PNs and quantities (of grain?)][via hbtin]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[m]ARAD-e₂SAG A ša₂ mi-dat-d60

(o 1) Arad-Reš, son of Idat-Anu

o 22

⸢m⸣dna-na-a-DU₃ *(P₂) mi-dat-d60 *(P₂) mki-tu₄1

(o 2) Nanaya-ibni | Idat-Anu | Kittu

o 33

[mina]-⸢E⸣-d60 *(P₂) mARAD-dMES re-bu-ut

(o 3) Ina-qibit-Anu | Arad-Marduk, one-fourth

o 44

[m]NIG₂.SUM.MU-d60 ma-ḫi-tu₄ PAP 12

(o 4) Nidintu-Anu, one-twelfth of a shekel, total of 12

o 55

[A]-MEŠ ša₂ md60-MU-GIŠ 22

(o 5) Sons of Anu-šum-lišir, 2

o 66

[A]-MEŠ ša₂ mki-tu-d60 2

(o 6) Sons of Kittu-Anu, 2

o 77

[m]ḫa-bu-na *(P₂) md60-AD-URI₃ ŠU.ḪA 2

(o 7) Habuna | Anu-ab-uṣur, a fisherman

o 88

[m]ki-din-d60 *(P₂) md60-MUnu 2

(o 8) Kidin-Anu | Anu-ittannu, 2

o 99

md60-⸢DIN⸣-su u mNIG₂.SUM.MU-d60 2

(o 9) Anu-bullissu and Nidintu-Anu, 2

o 1010

md60-DINiṭ ša₂-ʾa-ma-ʾa

(o 10) Anu-uballiṭ, purchasing

o 1111

mARAD-e₂re-eš qa ba? ḫa-ma?-at

(o 11) Arad-Reš, [...] one-fifth

o 1212

mza-ki-ʾi A ša₂ mdum-qi₂-d60

(o 12) Zaki, son of Dumqi-Anu

o 1313

mARAD-<d>MES A ša₂ mba-ga-e

(o 13) Arad-Marduk, son of Baga

o 1414

md60-DUMU-⸢MU⸣nu A ša₂ mDU₁₀.GA-d60

(o 14) Anu-mar-ittannu, son of Ṭab-Anu

o 1515

⸢m⸣d60-DIN-su A ša₂ md60-DUMU-MUnu

(o 15) Anu-bullissu, son of Anu-mar-ittannu

o 1616

[m]⸢d60⸣-MU-GIŠ u mi-da-a 2

(o 16) Anu-šum-lišir and Idaya, 2

o 1717

⸢m⸣u₂-bar

(o 17) Ubar

remainder uninscribed
reverse and sides uninscribed

1Throughout tablet, a glossenkeil (e.g. P₂) separates different individuals

2Beginning of lines 5-8 erased
